# Upcoming research & optimization

Forward-looking roadmap for the full GLM-5.2 / GB10 cluster. Three tracks: **faster decode** (JetSpec),
**bigger context** (NVFP4 weights + KV), and **the model itself** (REAP). Items are research directions,
not yet validated on sm_121a unless noted.

## 1. JetSpec — parallel-tree speculative decoding (the speed track)

*Plan auto-generated by GLM-5.2 itself (running locally on the 4×GB10 cluster, via the Hermes agent)
researching how to self-improve — lightly edited.*

**What it is:** [JetSpec](https://github.com/hao-ai-lab/JetSpec) (Hao AI Lab / UCSD, June 2026,
[paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18394)) trains a lightweight **draft head** on a *frozen* target.
The head proposes a whole **token tree in one parallel forward pass** (causal within each branch); the
target verifies the entire tree in one batched pass under a tree-causal mask. **Lossless** by construction.
Reported up to **9.64× on Qwen3-8B** (MATH-500, 1150 tok/s on B200); 7.1× HumanEval, 7.8× GSM8K.

**Why it matters here:** our single-stream decode is bandwidth-capped ~11-13 tok/s and DFlash was a dead
end (no GLM-5.2 drafter). JetSpec sidesteps that — you *train your own* draft head (small, ~0.9 GiB for a
35B-class head; only the head trains, target frozen). **No GLM-5.2 draft head is published**, so this is a
build, not a download.

**Phased plan:**
| Phase | Work | Est. |
|---|---|---|
| **1. Validate on GB10** | Clone + `pip install -e '.[bench,kernel]'`; run an *existing* Qwen3-8B head to confirm JetSpec's Triton tree-attention kernels + CUDA graphs work on sm_121a before investing | ~1 day |
| **2. Train a GLM-5.2 draft head** | Adapt `jetspec/models/` for GlmMoeDsa layer naming; pick `target_layer_ids` (which hidden states the head reads); forward-KL distillation vs GLM-5.2's own next-token dist | ~2-5 days |
| **3. Multi-node vLLM** | Integrate via the [vllm-jetspec](https://github.com/JetSpec-project/vllm-jetspec) fork; TP=4 (start single-node-4-GPU, then across nodes) | ~2-3 days |
| **4. Tune** | Sweep tree depth/width/budget per workload (code/math/chat) | ongoing |

**Known risks (flagged by the plan):**
- JetSpec's tree-attention kernels were **B200-tested** → expect sm_121a (GB10) kernel debugging, same class of work as our DSA mods.
- JetSpec's own engine is **single-node**; multi-node tree attention across the 200G fabric is **untested** — if inter-node overhead exceeds the spec-decode gain, run **JetSpec per-node (TP=4 each)** rather than TP=16 across all 4.
- **Quantized target**: verify the draft head can read hidden states from the **NVFP4/Int4-Int8 quantized** GLM-5.2 (precision differs from bf16) — test early.

## 2. Bigger context — NVFP4 weights + NVFP4 KV

- **`nvidia/GLM-5.2-NVFP4`** (full 753B, NVFP4 experts, FP8 KV, ~430-465 GB): lighter weights/node
  (~85-95 GB est. vs our 97 GB) → frees KV headroom. NVIDIA's flags are **8×B200 TP=8**; on sm_121a the
  realistic path is **b12x**: `--moe-backend b12x --attention-backend B12X_MLA_SPARSE --quantization
  modelopt_fp4 --hf-overrides index_topk_pattern=<F/S>` + `--decode-context-parallel-size 4`. The FP4-MoE
  segfault on sm_121a is already **broken by b12x** (proven on MiniMax-M3-NVFP4 on 4× DGX Spark).
  Plausible route to **128K** the full QuantTrio model can't reach.
- **Our `nvfp4_ds_mla` KV mod** (this repo, `patches/nvfp4-mla-kv/`) — already serving 100K. Stacks with
  the above. Next: NVFP4 the **DSA indexer cache** too (it's the un-quantized term that dominates per-token KV).

## 3. The model — REAP-504B (if VRAM is the wall)

- [`0xSero/GLM-5.2-504B`](https://huggingface.co/0xSero/GLM-5.2-504B): REAP expert-prune (256→168 routed
  experts/layer, ~290 GB) → frees ~24 GB/node. Reaps **only routed experts**; keeps shared-expert / MLA /
  DSA indexer / MTP. ~2-7% quality + looping (mitigate with `min_p`/`repetition_penalty`). **REAP-504B +
  our NVFP4-KV** is the credible route to true **128K-256K** on 4×GB10.

## 4. Near-term, low-risk
- **112K context** — the NVFP4 KV pool already allocates 118,720 tokens at util 0.87; raise max-model-len from 100K.
- **MTP-k sweep** {2,3,4,5}, `max-num-batched-tokens 4096`, thinking-off default — see [`OPTIMIZATION.md`](OPTIMIZATION.md).
